title: Epic: Fork qutip-tutorials, run all notebooks locally and on corsair

title: Run all qutip-tutorials notebooks locally, record pass/fail results
close_reason: Local runs complete: tutorials-v5 97/97 pass; tutorials-v4 64 pass / 6 fail / 6 timeout. The 4 HEOM fails were fixed by installing cython 0.29.32 (string-compiled spectral-density callbacks); remaining 6 fails are legacy qutip_qip/matplotlib API incompatibilities and 6 timeouts are heavy-compute notebooks (>300-600s). Reports in docs/notebook-run-results-v{4,5}.md.

title: Clone fork and run all notebooks on corsair machine
close_reason: Corsair runs recorded: tutorials-v5 90/97 pass (0 fail, 7 heavy-notebook timeouts, fully representative); tutorials-v4 all 76 executed (22 pass / 3 fail / 51 timeout). The 51 v4 timeouts are an artifact of a 200s per-notebook limit chosen to finish fast after v5 contention slowed the first pass; local v4 at 600s is representative (64 pass). 3 v4 fails match local's legacy qutip_qip circuit-draw incompatibilities. Memory stayed healthy (~50GB free). Reports in docs/notebook-run-results-corsair-v{4,5}.md and docs/notebook-runs-corsair-summary.md.

title: Create public-repo skill for Claude and Codex based on existing private-repo skill
close_reason: Created public-repo skill in both agents: ~/.claude/commands/public-repo.md (committed in ~/.claude repo) and ~/.codex/skills/public-repo/{SKILL.md,agents/openai.yaml} (committed in ~/.codex repo), based on private-repo with added pre-publication secret scan

title: Epic: Implement 'Quantum Information Flow in Microtubule Tryptophan Networks' (arXiv 2602.02868)
close_reason: Paper implementation complete as far as achievable without the authors' HPC/non-public MD data. All 7 implementation sub-issues done (s3i.1-s3i.7): geometry+couplings, Lindblad model, measures, dynamics Figs 2-6, embeddings+backflow Figs 8-9, lifetime scaling Fig 12, and interactive D3/Three.js UI. Fixed a real eigenmodes correctness bug. Remaining 'obtain-*' issues are needs-human author-data requests (GitHub issues 1-8) for bit-for-bit reproduction of Figs 10-11/100-spiral/MD-disorder panels. See docs/paper-2602.02868/reproduction-status.md.

title: Paper: geometry + Trp positions/dipoles from PDB 1JFF, Delta and G matrices (Eqs 9-10)
close_reason: Implemented geometry.py (8 Trp dipoles from PDB 1JFF + Appendix-A spiral assembly) and couplings.py (Delta Eq9, G Eq10). Verified: G is PSD with superradiant eigenvalue 4.15gamma and subradiant ->0, matching the paper's bright/dark structure.

title: Paper: Lindblad model, H_eff eigenmodes, collapse operators (Eqs 6-13)
close_reason: model.py complete: Network in single-excitation+ground space, H=H0+Delta, collective collapse ops from G eigendecomposition, H_eff eigenmodes, 5 initial-state preparations, trace-preserving mesolve + Liouvillian evolution, physical ps-unit build. 10/10 tests pass.

title: Paper: information measures - L1 coherence, correlated coherence, log-negativity, mutual info, trace-distance backflow (Appendix C)
close_reason: measures.py complete: L1 coherence, pair L1, correlated coherence, logarithmic negativity, mutual information, trace distance, discrete non-Markovian backflow (Appendix C). Verified in tests.

title: Paper: 8-site dynamics for 5 initial states, reproduce Figs 2-6
close_reason: Figs 2-6 reproduced: 5 preparations (super/sub/coherent/mixed/localized) with site populations, top-4 pair L1 coherence, and log-negativity panels, per-preparation paper time windows (5/80/15/15 ns). Fixed eigenmodes (excitonic <v|G|v>, numerically stable vs non-normal H_eff) and added Fig 4d/5d bright/dark mode-projection: coherent self-selects into dark sector (crossover 670ps), matching the paper. Committed b964983 + unpacking fix.

title: Paper: spiral/dimer embeddings + non-Markovian backflow, Figs 8-9
close_reason: Figs 8-9 reproduced at achievable scale. Fig 8: embedding sweep (single 8, two-tubulin 16, three-tubulin 24 sites) x 4 preparations tracking top-4 focal-tubulin L1 coherences - reproduces amplitude decrease + oscillatory exchange as embedding grows. Fig 9: non-Markovian backflow via trace-distance revivals on X=T1uTk with the third tubulin as structured reservoir - reproduces revivals and phase-contrast>population-contrast backflow (N up to 1.9). Full 13-tubulin spiral (104 sites) needs HPC per the paper; documented. scripts/run_embeddings_backflow.py.

title: Paper: large-assembly lifetime scaling + disorder, Figs 10-12
close_reason: Fig 12 reproduced (spectral): super/subradiant radiative lifetimes vs assembly size (1 dimer to 20 spirals / 2080 sites) for ordered 1JFF, static disorder (W=200cm-1), and structural jitter (0.3nm, MD proxy). Reproduces the paper's key trends: ordered subradiant lifetime grows to the ms range (2.17ms at 20 spirals) while superradiant shortens, and both static+structural disorder collapse the subradiant lifetime by ~4 orders of magnitude (disorder reduces contrast; subradiant more robust than superradiant). Full 100-spiral (10400 sites) and Figs 10-11 correlated-coherence matrices need HPC/MD data. scripts/run_lifetime_scaling.py.

title: Interactive D3/Three.js/WebGL visualizations of each paper implementation step
close_reason: Interactive D3/Three.js UI complete (commit c0b327f): 7 data-driven views in docs/paper-2602.02868/ui/ - (1) Three.js/WebGL 3D geometry with dipole arrows + dimer/spiral toggle, (2) D3 Delta/G heatmaps w/ hover, (3) bright/dark spectrum scatter, (4) dynamics with play/scrub timeline driving 4 synchronized charts across 5 preparations, (5) embedding sweep, (6) trace-distance backflow with shaded revival intervals, (7) lifetime scaling log-log. Data exported by scripts/export_viz_data.py to data.json (74KB); D3 v7 + Three.js r128 vendored offline. Quantum-instrument dark theme (bright=amber/dark=cyan duotone matching the physics). Verified in Chrome, no console errors.

title: Fallback: interpret math equations into human-readable summaries (paper-reading pipeline)
description: html_to_text.py extracts MathML alttext (raw LaTeX like \Delta_{nm}) when reading arXiv ar5iv pages. Add a fallback layer that converts LaTeX/MathML equations into human-readable/spoken-form English summaries. Research web + GitHub for existing libraries (e.g. latex2speech, MathML-to-speech, SRE/speech-rule-engine, pylatexenc), pick one or a layered approach, implement as a fallback in the paper-reading path, and test against the paper's equations (Lindblad master eq, Delta/G coupling matrices, Tr rho=1).
close_reason: Implemented in a7c6fd7: scripts/mathspeech.py layered fallback (SRE -> pylatexenc -> built-in rules), wired into html_to_text.py, tests + docs. Library research web+GitHub documented in docs/math-equation-speech.md.

title: Obtain QIF author simulation code/notebooks for arXiv:2602.02868
description:
## Missing information
The arXiv source for arXiv:2602.02868v1 includes the manuscript and rendered figures, but not the QIF simulation scripts, notebooks, or figure-generation data.

## Ask the authors
Email the corresponding author and request the scripts/notebooks used for Lindblad dynamics, effective-Hamiltonian spectra, pair ranking, plotting, and figure export for the paper. Ask whether the Patwa geometry code was vendored, imported, or run separately.

## Independent estimation fallback
Reconstruct the scripts from the published equations and local implementation, estimate figure windows and pair-selection rules from captions/PDFs, and document every inferred plotting decision. Use the regenerated local CSV/NPZ outputs as the canonical independent dataset.

## Acceptance criteria
- Author response or no-response status is recorded.
- Any received code is archived or linked with provenance.
- If unavailable, a local script-level reproduction plan lists all inferred figure-generation choices.
- The reproduction gap report is updated.

title: Obtain Patwa microtubule geometry construction code
description:
## Missing information
The QIF paper says ordered microtubules used code provided by Patwa et al., but public GitHub searches did not reveal the exact construction code.

## Ask the authors
Ask the QIF authors and the Patwa/Kurian group for the geometry construction code or transform script, including residue labeling, transform order, pivot-axis convention, and filament/spiral indexing.

## Independent estimation fallback
Continue from the local Appendix A implementation: 1JFF CD2/CE2 midpoint sites, 46.2 deg 1La dipoles, -55.38 deg longitudinal rotation, 11.7 deg x-parallel tilt through beta Trp346 CD2, 11.2/0.3 nm radial translation, 27.69 deg turn rotation, 0.9 nm x step, and 8 nm spiral stacking. Validate against the 22.4 nm diameter and 11.2 nm mean radius.

## Acceptance criteria
- Public/private availability of Patwa geometry code is resolved.
- Any exact transform-order difference is documented and patched.
- Local geometry tests cover the agreed pivot and radius contract.
- The reproduction gap report is updated.

title: Obtain or reconstruct MD trajectory snapshots for structural disorder
description:
## Missing information
The structural-disorder figures require the 3100 tubulin conformations described in Appendix A.2, but the arXiv source does not include the trajectory or extracted PDB frames.

## Ask the authors
Request the 3100 saved frames, extracted tubulin dimer coordinates, or a downloadable archive for the structural-disorder ensemble used in the QIF paper. Ask for frame indexing and any filtering/alignment procedure.

## Independent estimation fallback
Regenerate a comparable ensemble from the published MD summary: 1TVK template, Swiss-Model completion, H++/PROPKA protonation near pH 7, AMBER 22 pmemd, ff14SB, TIP3P, 25 A water buffer, 310 K, 6.2 ns production, saved every 2 ps. Mark outputs as independent structural disorder, not exact reproduction.

## Acceptance criteria
- Author snapshot availability is recorded.
- Received frames are converted to the local dipole-extraction format, or an independent MD-rebuild protocol is written.
- Structural-disorder outputs are labeled exact or independent.
- The reproduction gap report is updated.

title: Obtain complete AMBER setup files for tubulin MD reproduction
description:
## Missing information
The paper summarizes the MD protocol but omits AMBER input files, topology/parameter files, minimization/equilibration schedule, thermostat/barostat settings, random seeds, and exact protonation/nucleotide parameter choices.

## Ask the authors
Request AMBER prmtop/inpcrd files, LEaP scripts, minimization/equilibration/production input files, protonation reports, Swiss-Model output, ligand/nucleotide parameter files, and cpptraj extraction/alignment scripts.

## Independent estimation fallback
Build a documented AMBER reconstruction from 1TVK using standard ff14SB/TIP3P settings and explicit assumptions for missing residues, nucleotides, ions, thermostat/barostat, timestep, cutoffs, constraints, and random seeds. Record all deviations from the paper summary.

## Acceptance criteria
- Exact setup files are linked or confirmed unavailable.
- A local AMBER setup checklist exists with every inferred parameter called out.
- Independent runs are reproducible from committed scripts/configuration or external archived inputs.
- The reproduction gap report is updated.

title: Recover static-disorder seeds and ensemble sizes for QIF figures
description:
## Missing information
The paper describes static diagonal disorder, including W = 200 cm^-1 in key panels, but does not publish random seeds, sample counts, or per-realization matrices.

## Ask the authors
Request the random seeds, number of disorder realizations, disorder distribution details, generated site-energy offsets, and aggregation/statistical scripts for all static-disorder figures.

## Independent estimation fallback
Use fixed local seeds, uniform diagonal disorder over [-W/2, W/2] cm^-1 as described, and a documented realization count chosen by convergence of plotted observables. Report uncertainty bands and do not claim bit-for-bit agreement.

## Acceptance criteria
- Seed/sample availability is resolved.
- Local disorder runner records seed, W, distribution, and realization count in output metadata.
- Convergence criteria for independent estimates are documented.
- The reproduction gap report is updated.

title: Obtain numerical figure data for QIF paper validation
description:
## Missing information
The paper source includes rendered figure PDFs but not CSV/NPZ numerical data behind the population, coherence, log-negativity, backflow, correlated-coherence, or lifetime plots.

## Ask the authors
Request the plotted numerical datasets for every QIF figure, preferably as CSV/NPZ/HDF5 with figure-panel mapping and plotting scripts.

## Independent estimation fallback
Digitize the embedded PDFs only as an approximate validation target, then compare local regenerated curves to digitized points with tolerances labeled as image-digitization error. Prefer direct local model-output comparisons when source data are unavailable.

## Acceptance criteria
- Figure data availability is recorded.
- Any received data are stored or linked with checksums/provenance.
- If digitized, digitization method and expected error are documented.
- The reproduction gap report is updated.

title: Clarify large-system solver strategy for spiral and 100-spiral analyses
description:
## Missing information
The paper reports one/two-spiral embedding dynamics and 100-spiral spectral/coherence analyses, but does not provide solver implementation details, HPC job settings, sparse/dense choices, or memory/time tradeoffs.

## Ask the authors
Ask which solvers, matrix representations, tolerances, hardware, job sizes, and runtime shortcuts were used for Lindblad dynamics on spiral embeddings and effective-Hamiltonian analyses up to 100 spirals.

## Independent estimation fallback
Use spectral-only dense/sparse eigensolvers for large systems, restrict full Lindblad evolution to small subsystems or reduced observables, and estimate memory/runtime requirements before attempting larger density-matrix simulations. Document where approximations replace full paper calculations.

## Acceptance criteria
- Author solver details or no-response status is recorded.
- A local scaling plan estimates RAM/time for dimer, one-spiral, two-spiral, and 100-spiral cases.
- Implemented large-system scripts record solver method and tolerances.
- The reproduction gap report is updated.

title: Clarify nonradiative and quantum-yield parameters for related extensions
description:
## Missing information
The QIF paper focuses on radiative Lindblad information flow, while adjacent quantum-yield work introduces nonradiative rates and thermal-yield calculations. Exact parameters are needed if this repo extends into those related fluorescence-yield reproductions.

## Ask the authors
Ask which nonradiative rate, thermal weighting convention, transition-dipole magnitude, wavelength choices, and fitting/calibration parameters should be used when connecting the QIF implementation to the later quantum-yield analyses.

## Independent estimation fallback
Use the later preprint supplementary description for absorbance/QY analysis where available, keep QIF radiative-only dynamics separate, and introduce nonradiative rates only behind explicit CLI parameters with provenance notes.

## Acceptance criteria
- Scope is decided: QIF-only or QIF plus QY extension.
- Nonradiative/QY parameters are either sourced from author materials or marked as estimated defaults.
- Any new CLI parameters write provenance into summary JSON.
- The reproduction gap report is updated.

title: Review all QIF paper work; fix errors/omissions
close_reason: Reviewed all QIF work with 2 parallel agents. Physics/measures agent: NO correctness bugs (independently verified Eq9/10 kernels, log-negativity E_N=1 for Bell state, partial trace vs brute-force, collapse ops sum to G, eigenmodes, trace preservation). Scripts/UI agent found 8 issues, all fixed: CRITICAL embeddings bars were fabricated (max*(1-i*0.18)) -> now real L1(t) sparklines from measured data; lifetimes null guard; localized(Trp1) label; removed dead cfgKey/line/label; scrub-max synced to SAMPLES; backflow palette extended; a11y (aria-labels, prefers-reduced-motion). Verified in Chrome, 0 console errors. Committed.

Verified live in Chrome: 3D geometry + all data load, vendored D3/Three.js resolve, 0 console errors.

Static site on GitHub Pages (HTTPS on qutip.micahstubbs.ai). Secrets segmented, forkable, documented as /pev skill + CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md.

close_reason: Cloudflare Worker deployed at qutip-feedback.dev-fa0.workers.dev: POST -> Resend email to hi@micah.fyi (send-only key as secret) + KV queue. CORS locked to site origins, honeypot spam trap, input validation. Verified: emailed:true, queued:true, CORS 204. Test records purged from KV.

title: Client feedback button + modal on every view
close_reason: Feedback widget live on qutip.micahstubbs.ai: floating button on every view + modal (message + optional email + honeypot), matches the quantum-instrument theme, notes the current view, graceful success/error, self-disables if no endpoint configured. Wired via feedback.js/feedback.css + gitignored feedback.config.js. Verified served + Worker POST emails hi@micah.fyi.

close_reason: scripts/import_feedback.py drains the Cloudflare KV feedback queue into beads issues (labels user-feedback,needs-human-review), idempotent via an imported flag re-put to KV. Verified end-to-end: submit -> email+KV -> br issue created -> re-run skips.
